properties.remark: COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGER REPORTS A 30 TO 40 ACRE STAND OF TREES WAS FLATTENED ABOUT A HALF MILE TO THE EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF STATE HIGHWAY 100 AND HOLLAND-CHATTOOGAVILLE ROAD. THIS EXTENDES THE PATH OF THE PREVIOUSLY REPORTED TORNADO TO ABOUT 2.5 MILES TOTAL. NO EVIDENCE PRESENT TO INCREASE STRENGTH OVER THE PREVIOUS EF0 DESIGNATION.

2 MILE TRACK OF SPOTTY TREE DAMAGE ALONG OLD HOLLAND ROAD AND STATE HIGHWAY 100 IN THE HOLLAND COMMUNITY. LIGHT TO MODERATE DAMAGE TO THE ROOFS OF 3 BUILDINGS AND SEVERAL OUT BUILDINGS. DAMAGE APPEARED TO BE CAUSED BY A COMBINATION OF STRAIGHT LINE WINDS AND A BRIEF EF0 TORNADO.
ON HIGHWAY 100 ROOF BLOWN OFF A HOUSE, POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN.
AT THE FLOYD MEDICAL CENTER

POSSIBLE TORNADO REPORTED BY EMA DIRECTOR. PATH OF SPORADIC, MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE, BEGINNING AT 412 REEDY ROAD AND CONTINUING ACROSS U.S. 41 AND ENDING NEAR I-75. MINOR ROOF AND SIDING DAMAGE TO HOME ON REEDY ROAD, TREE ON A CAR, AND A 10 BY 12 FOOT BARN MOVED OFF ITS FOUNDATION. A TREE WAS ALSO DOWN ON RUMBLE ROAD FURTHER EAST.

TORNADO FORMED NEAR 412 REEDY CREEK RD...TRACKING EAST ACROSS US 41 NEAR THE SMARR WATER TOWER...THEN DISSIPATING AS IT CROSSED I-75 SOUTHEAST OF THE RUMBLE RD INDUSTRIAL PARK AT 303 AM. ONE HOME SUSTAINED MODERATE DAMAGE...PRIMARILY TO THE ROOF AND SIDING. 3 OTHER STRUCTURES HAD MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE. SPORADIC SNAPPED OR UPROOTED TREES WERE FOUND ALONG THE ENTIRE 3 MILE TORNADO PATH. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YDS. MAXIMUM WINDS ARE ESTIMATED AT 85 MPH.

POSSIBLE TORNADO. EMA DIRECTOR REPORTS SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE STARTING ON U.S. 441 AT MILE MARKER 2-3 AT TAPPIN SPUR ROAD. DAMAGE CONTINUES EAST TO HALE ROAD AND COLHAM ROAD. TREES DOWN AND A VEHICLE OVERTURNED. U.S. 441 WAS COMPLETELY CLOSED BECAUSE OF DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. ONE MOBILE HOME MOVED FROM ITS FOUNDATION.

TORNADO RATED EF1 AND 5 MILES IN LENGTH. SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE STARTING ON U.S. 441 AT MILE MARKER 2-3 AT TAPPIN SPUR ROAD. DAMAGE CONTINUES EAST TO HALE ROAD AND COLHAM ROAD. TREES DOWN AND A VEHICLE OVERTURNED. U.S. 441 WAS COMPLETELY CLOSED BECAUSE OF DOWNED TREES AND POWER LINES. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. ONE MOBILE HOME MOVED FROM ITS FOUNDATION. GEORGIA NATURE CENTER REPORTS HUNDREDS OF TREES DOWN...TWO TENT AREAS DESTROYED...DAMAGE TO SOLAR PANELS.
HALF DOLLAR SIZED HAIL REPORTED.
TWENTY TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED WITH AN APPROXIMATELY 50 ACRE WIDE AREA OF WOODS.

POSSIBLE TORNADO. EMA DIRECTOR REPORTS SIGNIFICANT PATH OF DAMAGE BEGINNING IN THE TYRONE COMMUNITY INT HE WESTERN PART OF THE COUNTY NEAR GEORGIA HIGHWAY 44 AND CONTINUING SOUTH TOWARD GEORGIA HIGHWAY 47. ONE 3500 SQ. FT. HOME COMPLETELY DEMOLISHED. FIVE OTHER BUILDINGS WITH MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE. A CHICKEN HOUSE WAS ALSO DESTROYED. NINE ROADS BLOCKED WITH DEBRIS, DOWNWED TREES, OR POWER LINES AT ONE TIME.

EF3 - BEGAN IN TYRONE IN FAR SOUTHWEST PART OF THE COUNTY AND CONTINUED ACROSS SOUTHERN WILKES INTO LINCOLN COUNTY. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND APPROXIMATELY 16 MILES WITHIN WILKES COUNTY. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 1/2 MILE. A CINDER BLOCK HOME WAS COMPLETELY DEMOLISHED AND THE DEBRIS WAS BLOWN 1/2 MILE DOWNSTREAM. FIFTEEN OTHER HOMES SUSTAINED MODERATE - MAJOR DAMAGE WITH 19 OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED. A COMMERCIAL CHICKEN HOUSE WAS DESTROYED...A STEEPLE BLOWN OFF A CHURCH...AND A 2-TON TRUCK MOVED 60FT

EF4 - BEGAN IN TYRONE IN FAR SOUTHWEST PART OF THE COUNTY AND CONTINUED ACROSS SOUTHERN WILKES INTO LINCOLN COUNTY. THE TORNADO WAS ON THE GROUND APPROXIMATELY 16 MILES WITHIN WILKES COUNTY. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 1/2 MILE. A CINDER BLOCK HOME WAS COMPLETELY DEMOLISHED AND THE DEBRIS WAS BLOWN 1/2 MILE DOWNSTREAM. FIFTEEN OTHER HOMES SUSTAINED MODERATE - MAJOR DAMAGE WITH 19 OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED. A COMMERCIAL CHICKEN HOUSE WAS DESTROYED...A STEEPLE BLOWN OFF A CHURCH...AND A 2-TON TRUCK MOVED 60FT

*** 1 INJ *** POSSIBLE TORNADO. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE FROM WILLARD TO EATONTON...ESPECIALLY ON THE WEST AND SOUTH SIDE OF THE CITY. ONE BUSINESS IN EATONTON COMPLETELY DESTROYED. DOZENS OF HOMES DAMAGED. ONE PERSON INJURED FROM DEBRIS. SOME PEOPLE BRIEFLY TRAPPED IN CARS AND HOMES. U.S. HIGHWAY 129 CLOSED FROM DOWNED TREES AND A MAJOR POWER LINE.

*** 1 INJ *** EF1 - SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE FROM WILLARD TO EATONTON ON A FIVE MILE LONG PATH. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 3/4 MILE. MAXIMUM WINDS ESTIMATED TO BE 110 MPH. ONE RESTAURANT COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND TWO OTHER BUILDINGS DESTROYED. NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED. AT LEAST ONE PERSON INJURED FROM DEBRIS. SOME PEOPLE BRIEFLY TRAPPED IN CARS AND HOMES. U.S. HIGHWAY 129 SOUTH OF EATONTON CLOSED FOR SEVERAL HOURS BY DOWNED POWER LINES.

*** 1 INJ *** SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE FROM WILLARD TO EATONTON...ESPECIALLY THE WEST AND SOUTH SIDE OF THE CITY. ONE RESTAURANT COMPLETELY DESTROYED. SEVERAL HOMES...BOTH MOBILE AND SITE-BUILT HOMES SUSTAINED MODERATE TO MAJOR DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES UPROOTED. AT LEAST ONE PERSON INJURED FROM DEBRIS. SOME PEOPLE BRIEFLY TRAPPED IN CARS AND HOMES. U.S. HIGHWAY 129 CLOSED BY DOWNED POWER LINES. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES AND PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 1/4 MILE WIDE.

EF1 - BEGINNING ONE MILE WEST OF SMITHBORO ALONG GEORGIA HIGHWAY 16...ENDING APPROXIMATLY TWO MILES EAST OF SMITHBORO. MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 1/4 MILE. DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG GEORGIA HIGHWAY 16 IN THE SMITHBORO COMMUNITY, ONE WHICH SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. A SAW MILL WAREHOUSE WAS DESTROYED. A NUMBER OF POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL.
1 HOME DAMAGED ON STONEY RIDGE ROAD.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. ROOF BLOWN OFF A MOBILE HOME. AT LEAST 12 TREES DOWN ON GEORGIA HIGHWAY 36 NEAR HENDERSON MILL ROAD. A BARN IN THE AREA WAS ALSO DESTROYED.

DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES ALONG GEORGIA HIGHWAY 16, ONE WHICH SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE NEAR THE END POINT. A NUMER OF POWER LINES AND TREES DOWN IN THE AREA AS WELL. JUST BEFORE THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN...MINOR DAMAGE WAS NOTED OUTSIDE A WAREHOUSE WHERE SUPPLIES WERE SCATTERED ABOUT. TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS ESTIMATED TO BE APPROXIMATLY SEVEN MILES LONG WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 1/4 MILE.

EF1 - TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT KING BOSTWICK RD. AND CHANNING COPE RD TRACKING APPROXIMATELY 2 MILES TO ABOUT 1/2 MI. SOUTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF HENDERSON MILL ROAD AND TAYLOR ROAD. PATH WIDTH ESTIMATED TO BE 100-200 YARDS WIDE. EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO 20 TO 30 HOMES IN A HEAVILY WOODED PINE COMMUNITY. DAMAGE WAS NEARLY 100 PERCENT CAUSED BY TREES DOWN ON HOMES.

EF1 - BEGINNING 9WNW SHADY DALE NEAR SHEPARD RD...ENDING 2NW SHADY DALE NEAR TUCKERTOWN RD. MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS - 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES AND MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 1/4 MILE. MINOR DAMAGE TO FIVE HOMES AND MAJOR DAMAGE TO A SMALL COTTAGE IN THE NORTHEAST PART OF THE COUNTY. MORE THAN 100 TREES DOWN ALONG THE PATH.

POSSIBLE TORNADO. TWELVE TO 15 HOMES DAMAGED SOUTH OF MORELAND NEAR THE MERIWETHER COUNTY LINE AND GEORGIA HIGHWAY 54...HINES ROAD...AND BEAR CREEK ROAD. A ROOF WAS BLOWN OFF A SCHOOL. THE DAMAGE WAS SOUTH OF GEORGIA HIGHWAY 16.

EF2 - PATH OF DAMAGE 15 MILES LONG BEGINNING AT 923 BRADBERRY ROAD THEN CONTINUED MOSTLY EASTWARD ACROSS FAR SOUTHERN COWETA COUNTY AND THEN CONTINUED INTO SPALDING COUNTY. PATH WIDTH UP TO ONE MILE WIDE. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES...ONE WITH ITS ROOF COMPLETELY OFF. HUNDREDS OF TREES DOWN ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. A HORSE WAS KILLED FROM FLYING DEBRIS NEAR U.S. 27A.

PATH OF DAMAGE SEVEN TO EIGHT MILES LONG BEGINNING AT 923 BRADBERRY ROAD...APPROXIMATELY THREE MILES WEST OF U.S. 27...THEN CONTINUING EAST-NORTHEAST ACROSS FAR SOUTHERN COWETA COUNTY. STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO AT LEAST A DOZEN HOMES...SOME SUBSTANTIAL. PATH WIDTH ESTIMATED TO BE 1 MILE WIDE AT SOME POINTS.

EF2 - PATH OF DAMAGE FROM THE COWETA/SPALDING COUNTY LINE...ABOUT 4 MILES EAST OF HARALSON...MOVING EASTWARD FOR APPROXIMATELY 4.5 MILES...ENDING APPROXIMATELY 9 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF GRIFFIN. THE TORNADO REMAINED JUST SOUTH OF GEORGIA HIGHWAY 16 THROUGHOUT. PATH WIDTH WAS 1/4 MI AT ITS WIDEST POINT. INTERMITTENT STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. FOUR HOMES COMPLETELY DESTROYED...WITH 40 SUSTAINING MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE. DOZENS OF TREES DOWN AS WELL.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN WITH ONE TREE DOWN ON A HOUSE.

*** 1 FATAL *** POSSIBLE TORNADO...SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AND A CHURCH DESTROYED IN THE HICKORY GROVE COMMUNITY. 1 FATALITY OCCURRED WHEN A PERSON WAS THROWN FROM HIS MOBILE HOME LOCATED IN THE HICKORY GROVE COMMUNITY. EVENT TIME WAS BASED ON RADAR.

*** 1 FATAL, 3 INJ *** EF3 - TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5 ESE MI. OF SPARTA AND CONTINUED TO INTO WARREN AND GLASCOCK COUNTY...ENDING 6 MILES WNW OF GIBSON. TOTAL PATH LENGTH 8 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 500 YARDS WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS OF 140 MPH. A CHURCH AND FOUR MOBILE HOMES DESTROYED IN THE HICKORY GROVE COMMUNITY. ONE FATALITY AND THREE INJURIES OCCURRED IN THE HICKORY GROVE COMMUNITY. HUNDREDS OF TREES DOWN ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.
*** 2 INJ *** POSSIBLE TORNADO. EMA DIRECTOR REPORTS THAT A HOME WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED EAST OF SPARTA BETWEEN THERE AND THE WARREN COUNTY LINE. TWO RESIDENTS IN THE HOME WERE INJURED.

*** 1 FATAL, 3 INJ *** EF3 - TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 5 ESE MI. OF SPARTA AND CONTINUED TO INTO WARREN...ENDING 0.25 MILE N OF SHOALS IN EXTREME SW WARREN COUNTY. TOTAL PATH LENGTH 8 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 500 YARDS WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS OF 140 MPH. A CHURCH AND FOUR MOBILE HOMES DESTROYED IN THE HICKORY GROVE COMMUNITY. ONE FATALITY AND THREE INJURIES OCCURRED IN THE HICKORY GROVE COMMUNITY. HUNDREDS OF TREES DOWN ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR SUMMERVILLE AND TRACKED EASTWARD ABOUT ONE MILE. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 200 YARDS AND MAXIMUM WINDS 120 MPH. TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR GEORGIA HIGHWAY 136 AND GRANDVIEW RD...JUST NORTHEAST OF JASPER AND TRACK EAST FOR ABOUT FOUR MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 1/4 MILE AND MAXIMUM WINDS 100 MPH. NUMEROUS 18-INCH AND LESS DIAMETER HARDWOOD TREES UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO. MANY POWER LINES DOWN AND SNAPPED UTILITY POLES AS WELL. ONE PERSON SUFFERED A HEART ATTACK DURING THE EVENT..BUT NO FATALITIES OR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.

NEARLY 100 LARGE HARDWOOD AND PINE TREES DOWN IN THE AREA. THREE BOATS DESTROYED...ONE SUNK AND ANOTHER THROWN SOME 200 YARDS OFF SHORE. AT LEAST THREE BOAT DOCKS DAMAGED...ONE DESTROYED.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES SOUTH OF OCHILLE TRACKED TO A POINT ABOUT 4 MILES TO THE SOUTHEAST. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 1/4 MILE AND MAXIMUM WINDS 100 MPH. TORNADO SPUNUP WITHIN A 2-MILE LONG AND TWO-MILE WIDE DOWNBURST PATH. COMBINED DAMAGED FROM THE DOWNBURST AND TORNADO CONSISTED OF DESTRUCTION OF SEVERAL BUILDINGS AND SHEDS. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE FLATTED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.

A SECOND TORNADO WAS SPAWNED FROM THE SAME THUNDERSTORM THAT EARLIER SPAWNED A TORNADO JUST SOUTH OF OCHILLE. THIS TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES EAST-NORTHEAST OF CUSSETA AND TRACKED EAST-SOUTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY FOUR MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 100 YARDS AND MAXIMUM WINDS 80 MPH. AT LEAST TWO HOMES AND ONE CHURCH WERE DAMAGED ALONG THE TORNADO PATH. SEVERAL SHEDS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.
POSSIBLE TORNADO
BRIEF TORNADO PATH OF APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES. MAXIMUM PATH WINDTH 100 FEET...WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 80 MPH. ONE MOBILE HOME LOST SHINGLES...SIEDING...AND SOME WINDOWS.

A SECOND TORNADO SPAWNED BY THE SAME THUNDERSTORM JUST A LITTLE FURTHER EAST. PATH LENGTH WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 1.1 MILES...MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH 100 FEET...AND MAXIMUM WINDS 80 MPH. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWN ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO.

*** 1 INJ *** THE SAME PARENT THUNDERSTORM THAT PREVIOUSLY CAUSED TWO BRIEF EF0 TORNADOES SPAWNED A THIRD AND MUCH STRONGER EF3 TORNADO ABOUT HALFWAY BETWEEN SPARTA AND CULVERTON. ONE 4000 SQ. FT. SITE-BUILT TWO-STORY HOME COMPLETELY DESTROYED WITH DEBRIS STREWN OVER 400 FT. ONE SERIOUS INJURY AT THIS HOME. ANOTHER MOBILE HOME WAS ALSO LEVELED...WITH MINOR DAMAGE TO TWO OTHER HOMES. A LARGE SWATH OF PECAN TREES IN AN ORCHARD WERE FLATTENED.

A TORNADO WITH EF-1 MAX INTENSITY TOUCHED DOWN 3 M ESE OF METHVINS IN FAR ERN SUMTER COUNTY AND TRAVELED ACROSS SRN DOOLY COUNTY BEFORE LIFTING IN FAR WRN WILCOX COUNTY ABOUT 5 MI N OF SEVILLE AROUND 1145 PM. TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS 25 MI AND MAX PATH WIDTH WAS 250 YARDS. MAX WIND SPEEDS WERE 110 MPH. AROUND 1000 TREES TOTAL DOWNED. TWO HOMES WITH SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE...ONE FROM A FALLEN TREE. SEVERAL BARNS AND OUTBUILDINGS DESTROYED. SEVERAL IRRIGATION SYSTEMS ROLLED OVER.
SEVERAL PECAN TREES UPROOTED
APPROX 10 TREES DOWN

ANOTHER TORNADO RATED EF-0 TOUCHED DOWN 1 MI NORTH OF VIENNA AND TRAVELED 6 MI WITH SPOTTY DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING 6 MI ENE OF VIENNA AROUND 1128 PM. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED SIMULTANEOUSLY WITH THE EF-1 TORNADO OVER SOUTHERN DOOLY COUNTY. A CHICKEN HOUSE WAS DESTROYED. MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE HOME. 15 TO 20 TREES DOWNED OR SNAPPED. TIN ROOF PEELED OFF BARN.
POSSIBLE TORNADO DESTROYED A CHICKEN HOUSE AND DOWNED SEVERAL TREES NEARBY. THIS AREA WILL BE SURVEYED BY NWS PERSONNEL ON WEDNESDAY.
POSSIBLE TORNADO CAUSED EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES AND SNAPPED DOZENS OF TREES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PARHAM RD AND LAMBSTOWN RD. THIS AREA WILL BE SURVEYED BY NWS PERSONNEL ON WEDNESDAY.

ISOLATED DAMAGE FROM POSSIBLE BRIEF TORNADO NEAR LOGUE AVE IN GIBSON. OUTBUILDING DESTROYED AND PORCH RIPPED OFF HOUSE. FOUR TREES DOWN. NO DAMAGE TO SURROUNDING AREAS.

TORNADO BEGAN IN THE COBB COMMUNITY IN FAR SOUTHEAST SUMTER COUNTY AND CROSSED INTO CRISP COUNTY ACROSS LAKE BLACKSHEAR. TOTAL TORNADO PATH WAS 23 MILES THROUGH SUMTER AND CRISP COUNTIES. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH ESTIMATED TO BE 200 YARDS WITH MAXIMUM WINDS 100 MPH. WITHIN SUMTER COUNTY...45 HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND ONE WAS DESTROYED. DAMAGE WAS LARGELY THE RESULT OF FALLEN LARGE PINE TREES. AROUND 100 TREES AND MANY POWER LINES WERE DOWN ALONG THE 23-MILE LONG TORNADO PATH THROUGH THE TWO COUNTIES.

LIKELY TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN NEAR HWY 80 AND HWY 296 AT HEARTSGROVE BAPTIST CHURCH. PART OF ROOF AND BRICK SIDING AND CHIMNEY FROM CHURCH REMOVED. TIPPED OVER SEVERAL GRANITE HEADSTONES IN ADJACENT CEMETARY. NUMEROUS TREES DOWNED ALONG 3/4 MI TRACK EAST OF CHURCH.
POSSIBLE TORNADO
POSSIBLE TORNADO BLEW ROOFS OFF HOUSES AND KNOCKED DOWN UNKNOWN NUMBER OF TREES AND POWER LINES.

TORNADO THAT BEGAN NEAR COBB IN SOUTHEAST SUMTER COUNTY CROSSED LAKE BLACKSHEAR AND INTO CRISP COUNTY AND CONTINUED ON A SPORADIC PATH ACROSS THE CENTRAL PART OF THE COUNTY TO HATLEY...JUST SHY OF THE WILCOX COUNTY LINE. MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 200 YARDS WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 100 MPH. ALTHOUGH THE TORNADO ENTERED THE COUNTY AS AN EF1...IT DIMINISHED TO AN EF0 AS IT CROSSED THE LAKE. TWENTY HOMES WERE DAMAGED ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO...AGAIN MOSTLY FROM DOWNED TREES.

WATER FLOWING OVER ROADWAY ON SALACOA ROAD.
BISHOP CREEK OUT OF ITS BANKS AND THREATENING HOMES IN BROOKS FARM AREA
2 TREES DOWN ON VETERANS PARKWAY NEAR COOPERS CREEK ROAD
AT LEAST 15-20 TREES DOWN IN SANDERSVILLE AROUND THE 911 CENTER. A COUPLE OF TREES DOWN ON BUSINESSES...AND A METAL CARPORT WAS BLOWN DOWN.
AT LEAST A DOZEN TREES BLOWN DOWN OR SNAPPED..AND A FEW POWER LINES DOWN.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ON MCLITTLE BRIDGE ROAD
SOUTH WALL OF A GROCERY STORE COLLAPSED ON HIGHWAY 19.
TREE FELL ON A HOUSE ON HIGHWAY 19.
ROOF DAMAGE ON A HOUSE ON HIGHWAY 19 SOUTH OF MCLITTLE BRIDGE RD

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 700 PM APPROX 5 NM S OF AMERICUS NEAR US HWY 19. CAUSED WALL TO COLLAPSE AND OTHER DAMAGE TO GROCERY STORE AT 1146 S HWY 19 SOUTH OF MCLITTLE BRIDGE RD. LARGE TREE FELL ON A FORESTRY BUILDING NEAR THIS LOCATION. ONE HOME WAS ALSO DAMAGED. AT LEAST 50 TO 100 TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE 2 MILE PATH BEFORE LIFTING AT HWY 377 APPOX 5.5 SSE OF AMERICUS AROUND 708 PM. MAX PATH WIDTH 50 YARDS. MAX WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH.

LIKELY TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 700 PM APPROX 5 NM S OF AMERICUS NEAR US HWY 19. CAUSED WALL TO COLLAPSE AND OTHER DAMAGE TO GROCERY STORE AT 1146 S HWY 19 SOUTH OF MCLITTLE BRIDGE RD. LARGE TREE FELL ON A FORESTRY BUILDING NEAR THIS LOCATION. ONE HOME WAS ALSO DAMAGED. AT LEAST 50 TO 100 TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE 2 MILE PATH BEFORE LIFTING 5.5 NM S OF AMERICUS AROUND 708 PM. THE TORNADO HAS NOT YET BEEN RATED PENDING FURTHER INVESTIGATION. INITIAL WINDS EST AT 100 MPH.
TREE TOPS AND POWER LINES DOWN

LIKELY TORNADO. SOME MINOR HOME DAMAGE. SOME TREE TOPS WERE BROKEN OFF. THERE WAS A RELATED CAR CRASH WITH INJRIES AT THE INTERSECTION OF GA 87 AND ROCK QUARRY ROAD. DAMAGE STARTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GOSE AND CALLAWAY ROAD.

MINOR DAMAGE TO 2 HOMES INCLUDING DAMAGE TO SIDING... GUTTERS AND TELEVISION ANTENNAE. TWO OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. 5 PINE TREES HAD TOPS BROKEN OFF. THERE WAS A RELATED CAR CRASH WITH INJRIES AT THE INTERSECTION OF GA 87 AND ROCK QUARRY ROAD. DAMAGE STARTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GOSE AND CALLAWAY ROAD.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. SOME TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN EAST OF JULIETTE.

EF0 TORNADO WITH WINDS AROUND 80 MPH OCCURRED ON THE PIEDMONT WILDLIFE REFUGE. 50 TO 100 TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG A PATH 2 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE. TORNADO PATH BEGAN 2 MILES NNW OF THE JARRELL PLANTATION AND ENDED 3 MILES NORTH OF THE JARRELL PLANTATION.
POSSIBLE TORNADO. TREES DOWN ALONG HWY 11 SOUTH, FULLERTON PHILLIPS, AND WENUT. TOTAL NUMBER UNKNOWN AT THIS TIME.

A WEAK TORNADO RATED EF-0 WITH WINDS UP TO 85 MPH TOUCHED DOWN IN A FORESTED AREA APPROXIMATELY 4.3 MILES WEST OF EATONTON. THE TORNADO TRAVELED ABOUT 1.5 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS BEFORE LIFTING ABOUT 3.5 MILES WEST-NORTHWEST OF EATONTON AT 158 PM EST. AT LEAST 500 TREES WERE DOWNED WITH AROUND 50 OF THOSE DOWNED ACROSS FORESTRY ROADS. TWO HUNTERS WERE TRAPPED BUT UNHARMED IN THIS AREA AND WERE NOT FREED UNTIL NEARBY ROADS WERE CLEARED.

TORNADO RATED EF-1 WITH WINDS TO 90 MPH TOUCHED DOWN NEAR LITTLE RD AND HARMONY RD APPROX 8.2 MILES NNE OF EATONTON. THE TORNADO TRAVELED ENE 1.5 MILES WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 100 YARDS BEFORE LIFTING 9 MILES NNE OF EATONTON AT 208 PM. HUNDREDS OF TREES AND SEVERAL POWER LINES WERE DOWNED. A MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED ONTO ITS SIDE. THE OCCUPANT WAS UNINJURED BUT TAKEN TO THE HOSPITAL DUE TO A CORONARY EPISODE. AT LEAST ONE OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED. TIN ROOFING OFF A HOME WAS PULLED OFF.

STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ACCELERATED OVER THE NORTHERN END OF LAKE SINCLAIR WITH SPEEDS UP TO 80 MPH CAUSING DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME ON TWISTING HILL DR. A LOOSELY ATTACHED SCREENED PORCH WAS RIPPED OFF THE HOME AND THROWN 20 YARDS. APPROX 20 TREES WERE ALSO DOWNED IN THIS AREA.
BARN WAS BLOWN ACROSS THE FIELD ON CLARK GRESHAM ROAD.
TWO TREES DOWN IN LEXINGTON.
ONE TREE DOWN.

ONE TREE DOWN ON CLOUDLAND ROAD NEAR MENLO AND ONE DOWN ON HWY 100 NEAR SUMMERVILLE.
EAST LAKE VIEW DRIVE AND MACK SMITH ROAD IN ROSSVILLE ARE CLOSED DUE TO WATER ON ROADWAY.

ONE TREE DOWN NEAR TRENTON.
4-5 TREES DOWN AND A FEW POWERLINES DOWN ACROSS NORTHERN FLOYD COUNTY.

911 INDICATED NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND FENCES BLOWN OVER. NO SPECIFIC INFORMATION IS AVAILABLE AT THIS TIME.

FRAZIER DRIVE WAS WASHED OUT BY A STREAM.
DROWNING BEAR CREEK WASHED OUT VINEWOOD DRIVE. THE BASEMENT OF A HOME NEAR BY WAS FLOODED.
STOVER CREEK WASHED OUT STOVER CREEK ROAD. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO THREE...SIX FOOT DIAMETER PIPES. WHITFIELD COUNTY MOBILE EMERGENCY COMMAND CENTER WILL BE ON THE SEEN THROUGHOUT THE NIGHT.

SNAKE CREEK CAME OUT OF ITS BANKS AND FLOODED BAUGH MOUNTAIN ROAD.
TREE DOWN ON A HOUSE ON OGLETHORPE STREET WITH PEOPLE TRAPPED INSIDE. SEVERAL REPORTS OF FUNNEL CLOUDS.

FLOODING OF CEDAR CREEK CAUSED CEDAR LAKE TO OVERFLOW AND THREATEN SEVERAL HOMES IN THE CEDAR LAKE SUBDIVISION. THE WATER LEVEL ROSE UP INTO CRAWL SPACES OF THE HOMES AND COVERED THE RESIDENTIAL ROADS.
FLOODING ON PARKERS MILL CREEK WAS CAUSING OVERTOPPING OF LAKE COLLINS DAM. WATER WAS FLOWING OVER PORTIONS OF U.S. HIGHWAY 19...RAWLEY ROAD AND MORRIS DRIVE NEAR THE LAKE.
FLOODING ON MILL CREEK WAS CAUSING OVERTOPPING OF MURPHY MILL DAM. THE WATER WAS OVERFLOWING MURPHY MILL ROAD AND TWO PEOPLE WERE RESCUED FROM THEIR CAR BY EMERGENCY OFFICIALS.
FLOODING ON WOLF CREEK WAS CAUSING OVERTOPPING OF THE MCMATH MILL POND DAM. WATER WAS FLOWING OVER MCMATH MILL ROAD AND IT WAS CLOSED.
